ops.title: Triage judge: tool schemas in the prefix are load-bearing (not just cost)
ops.body:
The deployed cc-steer triage judge is `cc_transcript.judge.structured_judge(Verdict, tier)` → `spawnllm.extract` → `RunSpec(agent=False, isolated=True, response_model=Verdict)` → `claude -p --model sonnet --system-prompt "" --json-schema <Verdict> --output-format json`. It is a SINGLE-SHOT structured extract (not a multi-turn agentic loop), but it carries NO `--tools` flag, so the CLI's ~52K tokens of built-in tool schemas load into the (cached) prompt prefix even though the judge never calls a tool. Cost ≈ $0.083/call, ~85% cache-served (1-hour prompt cache).

NON-OBVIOUS: those unused tool schemas are LOAD-BEARING for the judge's verdicts. Stripping them (lean invocation: `ClaudeConfig(tools="")` + system/user split + `MAX_THINKING_TOKENS=0`) drops input to ~9-12K tokens AND drops auditor agreement 0.84 → 0.74-0.78 (measured E34 lean-prep + E30.7). The schemas act as implicit context that shifts sonnet's steering calls. So you CANNOT cut judge cost by dropping the schemas without silently changing the judge — the "scaffolding" is part of the prompt, not just overhead.

Consequences: (1) every Wave-0 experiment scoring the judge (E33/E34/E35) must use the faithful scaffolded `isolated=True` invocation; lean is invalid. (2) Faithful cost is ~$0.083/call, ~6x the plan's implicit estimate → the plan's $80 for E33+E34+E35 is ~6x short. (3) A real production question for later: is paying 52K tool-schema tokens per triage call (on every steering moment) acceptable, given the schemas are never exercised but ARE load-bearing? A retuned v-next prompt that doesn't depend on them would be much cheaper — but that's a redesign, out of this campaign's scope. Trace cites: cc_steer/cli.py:313, triage.py:414-423, cc_transcript/judge/llm.py:70-72, spawnllm backends/claude.py:85-143.
